name: 📋 Tasks
description: Built-in Kanban tracker. Attach screenshots, link tasks, dispatch to agents inside git worktrees, set pre/post hooks.
author: reflex-org

slashCommands.label: /task
slashCommands.description: File a new task on the Kanban board.
slashCommands.promptBlock:
## /task — file a card on the Kanban board

The user wants a new task on the board. Parse their message as the title (and possibly description after the first newline / dash). Default `type: "feature"` unless context says otherwise — bug if they mentioned 'fix' or 'broken', research if 'find out' / 'investigate', call if 'meeting' / 'звонок'.

Emit ONE `<<reflex:task-create>>` marker, then a 1-line confirmation:

```
<<reflex:task-create>>{"title":"<derived>","body":"<context from message + any criteria>","type":"feature|bug|research|...","status":"backlog","priority":"normal"}<</reflex:task-create>>
```

Don't dispatch yet — just file. The user can drag it to `ready` and click Send to agent on the board.

Payload: {payload}

slashCommands.label: /tasks
slashCommands.description: Show what's on the board right now.
slashCommands.promptBlock:
## /tasks — show board snapshot

Use `reflex.kb.list({kind: "task"})` (or read the project's `.reflex/task/` directory) to gather every task. Summarise in a compact table grouped by column (Backlog / Ready / In-progress / Review / Done / Blocked), with `id`, `title`, `priority`. If the user passed a payload, filter tasks by case-insensitive substring in title or labels. Reply in {language}.

Don't dispatch anything; this is a read-only snapshot. Suggest a next action only if it's clearly useful ("3 ready tasks; want me to take the top one?").

slashCommands.label: /take-task
slashCommands.description: Pick the next task from `ready` and dispatch it to an agent.
slashCommands.promptBlock:
## /take-task — dispatch the next task

If the payload is a task id, use that. Otherwise pick the highest-priority task in the `ready` column (ties broken by oldest `createdAt`). Skip anything currently `in-progress`.

Emit `<<reflex:task-update>>{"id":"<id>","patch":{"status":"in-progress"}}<</reflex:task-update>>` to flip status. Then tell the user one line: "Picking task <id>: <title>. Opening worktree…" — the task-board utility will detect the status flip and call `reflex.tasks.dispatch` to wire up the topic + worktree.

Reply in {language}.

workflows.label: Task board · auto-pickup
workflows.description: Scans the `ready` column and dispatches the next task to an agent. Settings → Tasks → Enable auto-pickup to activate.

extensions.systemPromptAddendum:
## Task tracker installed

This Space has the task-board utility installed. The agent can:

- File a card via `<<reflex:task-create>>{title, body, type, status?, priority?, labels?}<</reflex:task-create>>`. Use freely whenever the user surfaces a piece of work bigger than this turn.
- Update a card via `<<reflex:task-update>>{id, patch:{status?, body?, priority?}}<</reflex:task-update>>`. Especially when you're inside a topic dispatched FROM a task — flip to `status: "done"` once the work lands.
- Types: `feature`, `bug`, `refactor`, `docs`, `chore`, `research`, `review`, `call`, `idea`. Statuses: `backlog`, `ready`, `in-progress`, `review`, `done`, `blocked`.
- The user can drag cards across columns on the board UI. Tasks that touch code get an isolated git worktree on dispatch — agents working in parallel never collide on files.
